commit.message:
Fix Azure CI by specifying Node.js 20.x version (#1626)

The CI pipeline was failing because it didn't specify a Node.js version,
relying on the pre-installed version on the build image (likely Node 18.x).
The latest @vscode/vsce uses undici which requires the File global API,
only available in Node.js 20+. This caused 'ReferenceError: File is not
defined' during vsce package.

Add NodeTool@0 task with Node 20.x to align with the GitHub workflow and
other Azure pipelines (nightly.yml, rc.yml) which already specify Node 20.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(seo): migrate previousSlugs to locale-aware previousSlugsByLocale

PROBLEM: previousSlugs was a flat array with no locale context. When a
job's FR slug changed but the old slug matched the active DE slug, the
safety nets removed it — causing 404s for /fr/trouver-emploi-tessin/<old-slug>.

SOLUTION: New locale-aware format previousSlugsByLocale: { fr: [...], de: [...] }
with per-locale safety nets that only strip matches within the same locale.

Core changes:
- 6 helper functions in dedicated-crawler-common.mjs:
  addPreviousSlugForLocale, getPreviousSlugsForLocale, getAllPreviousSlugs,
  cleanPreviousSlugsPerLocale, syncLegacyPreviousSlugs, mergePreviousSlugsByLocale
- Rewrote captureLostSlugs() to write per-locale
- Fixed all 4 safety nets (hardenJobLocaleFields, writeJobsCrawlerSlice,
  regenerate-slugs-by-locale, cleanPreviousSlugsPerLocale)
- Fixed all write points (mergePreserveLocaleData, preferJob)
- Fixed build plugin bridge page generation (locale-aware)
- Fixed runtime consumers (JobBoard.tsx, router.ts, localeJobsSplitPlugin)
- Updated scatter-jobs, find-orphan, assemble-jobs-dataset

Data migration:
- Recovered 106 previousSlugs lost in commit 7723f77f1 cleanup
- Migrated 3680 flat previousSlugs to locale-aware format across 111 files
- Legacy flat previousSlugs kept in sync for backward compatibility

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: improve scoring reliability — error isolation, retry limits, name matching (#67)

* fix: improve scoring reliability — error isolation, retry limits, name matching

- Wrap each match scoring in try-catch so one failure doesn't crash entire cron
- Add 10-attempt limit — permanently fail matches after max retries
- Guard against empty performances: fail (not sync) if no batting data extracted
- Guard against all-unmatched: skip league scoring if all player names fail to match
- Store unmatched_names in fantasy_scores.breakdown for debugging
- Log unmatched player names in cron output with match/league context
- Add markFailed() helper for consistent error tracking in cricket_sync_tracker
- Filter out permanently failed matches (>=10 attempts) from loadPendingMatches query

Name matching improvements:
- Add deep normalization: strip dots, hyphens, apostrophes before matching
- Handle short last names (<4 chars): try full-string contains and unique last name match
- Add 2-char first name prefix fallback when initial matching narrows to 0

Dismissal detection:
- Check additional CricAPI field names: mode, how_out, wicketCode, out_desc

Fallback improvements:
- Try Cricsheet cache on ANY CricAPI error (not just rate-limit)
- Covers HTTP 500, timeouts, network errors — only re-throw if both providers fail

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review — atomic attempt increment, lookup tracker for all matches

- Look up attempt count from cricket_sync_tracker for newly discovered matches
  (not just pendingById) to prevent bypassing the max-attempts check
- Use atomic increment RPC (increment_sync_attempts) to avoid race condition
  when cron runs concurrently with manual triggers
- Add migration 017 for the increment_sync_attempts RPC

commit.message:
Async trie prefetch: overlap CPU trie I/O with GPU inner steps

- Wrap MemorySystem in Arc<MemorySystem> to enable shared ownership across threads
- Add PrefetchHandle: background trie read via mpsc channel
- Add compute_seed_addrs(): pass-1 forward + AddrNet → CPU byte addresses
- Add spawn_prefetch_read(): dispatches trie read to background thread
- Add extract_write_data(): GPU v_proj + AddrNet → CPU-serializable write data
- Add spawn_async_write(): fire-and-forget background trie write thread
- Phase B/C: pre-compute addresses for next step, spawn trie read before inner loop
- Phase B/C: async trie write after inner loop (non-blocking)

Expected speedup: 1.5-2.5x at inner_steps=16 (partial overlap),
fully pipelined at inner_steps>=20 where GPU time >= trie read time.
GPU utilization target: 60-80% (up from ~25%).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(seo): align sitemap previousSlug paths with bridge page locale paths

The sitemap generator was adding ALL previousSlugs under the Italian
path (/cerca-lavoro-ticino/), but the bridge page generator correctly
places locale-specific previousSlugs under their locale prefix
(e.g. /en/find-jobs-ticino/, /fr/trouver-emploi-tessin/).

This mismatch caused 4 sitemap URLs to reference pages that didn't
exist in dist/, blocking deploy at the validate-sitemap-links step.

Fix: Rewrite the sitemap previousSlug section to mirror the bridge
page generator's locale-aware logic:
- previousSlugsByLocale[locale] entries → locale-specific path
- Legacy flat previousSlugs → Italian path (matching bridge behavior)
- Added deduplication via prevSlugSitemapPaths set

* fix: normalize warning_pct to 2 decimal places for lossless UI round-trip

The frontend form uses integers 0-100 but the backend stores 0-1 decimal.
Values with excess precision (e.g., 0.3333) would lose data on edit via
Math.round(). Adding a Pydantic field_validator with ROUND_HALF_UP on both
LimitCreate and LimitUpdate ensures the stored value always maps cleanly
to a whole percentage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: draw continuous average trend line across sparse historical data

Use null instead of omitting the average property so Recharts
connectNulls draws a continuous dashed line through gaps. Also guard
the tooltip against null values to avoid showing misleading \.

Adds backend test documenting _average_series behavior with empty
periods (months with zero expenses are excluded from the average).

feat(research): CR improvement & meal analysis (EXP-1871–1878)

Profile CR is 38% too high for ALL 11 patients — patients need ~60% more
insulin per carb than profiles suggest. AID loop compensates with +2.0U
extra IOB post-meal, masking the miscalibration.

EXP-1871: BOTH_TIMING_AND_MAGNITUDE — model underpredicts rise by 134 mg/dL
EXP-1872: UNPREDICTABLE — per-gram excursion CV=1.17, large meals saturate
EXP-1873: PRE_BOLUS_REDUCES_EXCURSION — 22 mg/dL improvement (9/10)
EXP-1874: CR_SIGNIFICANTLY_MISCALIBRATED — 38% too high (11/11) ⭐
EXP-1875: MODERATE_CIRCADIAN_CR — 28% range, 9/11 breakfast < evening
EXP-1876: LOOP_ACTIVELY_COMPENSATES — 83% active, +2.0U post-meal
EXP-1877: UAM_MORE_SYSTEMATIC — announced RMSE 3.4× larger than UAM
EXP-1878: CR_ESTIMATOR_IMPROVES — equation method +20%, wins 7/11

Key insight: wrong CR poisons the entire model during meal windows.
Announced meal RMSE (39.3) is 3.4× UAM RMSE (11.6) because wrong CR
contaminates the supply/demand model. Fix CR > detect UAM.

Equation-based CR estimator: CR = carbs × ISF / (excursion + bolus × ISF)
generalizes temporally and improves 19.8% over profile.

1 experiment script, 1 report, 8 figures

EXP-1863: CONSTANT_ISF_SUFFICIENT — Hill ISF worsens S/D for 8/9 patients
EXP-1864: DOSE_PARTIAL_EXPLANATION — dose explains 22% of ISF variance
EXP-1865: SMB_HIGHLY_EFFICIENT — 4.6× more efficient per unit ⭐

EXP-1867: NO_IMPROVEMENT — Hill ISF in S/D model worsens 7/9
EXP-1868: DOES_NOT_GENERALIZE — only 2/10 improve on held-out data

Key insight: Hill ISF describes EVENT-LEVEL dose-response at corrections,
not the CONTINUOUS insulin sensitivity coefficient the S/D model needs.
These share units (mg/dL/U) but measure different quantities.

Most actionable: SMBs deliver 4.6× more glucose drop per unit than larger
boluses. Total drop from 5U is only 1.7× a 1U dose. Strongest data-driven
justification for SMB dosing strategy.

commit.message:
fix: replace DEFAULT_VARIANT_SENTINEL with asset-name template matching

The sentinel approach was broken for packages like uv and copilot-cli
where extract_variant_from_asset returns non-None values (e.g. '64', 'apple',
'gh-copilot') for default binaries due to imperfect platform pattern stripping.

Replace with normalize_asset_for_matching() which strips version numbers
and compares normalized asset names directly, bypassing extract_variant_from_asset
entirely for the no-named-variant update case.

Fixes: 'Variant (default) no longer available' error for uv, copilot-cli,
ripgrep, and any other package with unusual asset naming.

commit.message:
fix: switch imageService from cloudflare-binding to passthrough (#72)

* Initial plan

* fix: change imageService from cloudflare-binding to passthrough

The IMAGES Worker binding (env.IMAGES) is not provisioned — Cloudflare
Images is a paid add-on that has not been enabled for this account.
Using 'cloudflare-binding' caused env.IMAGES to be undefined at runtime,
which broke env.ASSETS.fetch() and produced a 'Page not found' fallback
on every request.

'passthrough' skips image optimisation entirely and passes images through
unchanged, which is the correct behaviour for a static site served from
Workers Assets where Cloudflare Images is not provisioned.

Updated the comment above imageService to document:
- why 'passthrough' is used
- what is required before switching to 'cloudflare-binding' in future

No IMAGES binding exists in src/types/env.ts — no change needed there.

commit.message:
fix: merge project ini over defaults

Project-level hammocking.ini via --config completely replaced the
built-in defaults instead of overlaying. This caused critical settings
like exclude_pattern to be lost, breaking builds with GCC/MinGW where
runtime symbols like _pei386_runtime_relocator leaked through.

- Add HammockIni.merge() to overlay non-None values on top of defaults
- HammockRunner always loads package defaults first, then project config
- Extend default exclude_pattern with memcpy, memmove, memset, memcmp,
  bzero, strlen (compiler-generated intrinsics)
- Rewrite docs/usage.md: complete CLI reference, config merge docs,
  output styles, real build examples
- Add NmWrapper.mock_it() unit tests for symbol filtering logic
